title: Ensuring Citizenship Verification and Integrity in Federal Elections
summary: Executive Order 14399, titled "Ensuring Citizenship Verification and Integrity in Federal Elections," mandates enhanced measures to verify voter eligibility in U.S. Federal elections. The order directs the Department of Homeland Security, in coordination with the Social Security Administration, to compile and transmit "State Citizenship Lists" to state election officials, comprising U.S. citizens eligible to vote. This list will be derived from Federal citizenship and naturalization records, SSA data, and the Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE) program. The order also instructs the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to initiate rulemaking for mail-in and absentee ballots, requiring them to be sent in envelopes marked as "Official Election Mail" with unique barcodes for tracking. The USPS will maintain a "Mail-In and Absentee Participation List" for each state. The Attorney General is tasked with prioritizing investigations and prosecutions related to election fraud, including the issuance of ballots to ineligible individuals. The order emphasizes the importance of maintaining public confidence in election outcomes and enforcing Federal law to prevent non-citizens from voting in Federal elections.
